The genus Wikstroemia is a group of small shrubs or trees native to Asia and the Pacific. Wikstroemia austrocochinchinensis is specifically found in Vietnam and China. Within the genus Wikstroemia, W. austrocochinchinensis is a species that belongs to the class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Wikstroemia austrocochinchinensis, also known as Vietnamese Wikstroemia, is a medicinal plant species in the family Thymelaeaceae, traditionally used in Vietnamese and Southeast Asian herbal medicine. It has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Wikstroemia austrocochinchinensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Wikstroemia austrocochinchinensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may be combined with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
